text:
Jack loves collecting seashells. Every weekend, he hurries to the seaside, armed with a small bucket and an insatiable appetite for shells. On windy days, waves crash against the shore, scattering treasures in all directions.

One spring day, while Jack was wandering the sandy beach, he found something extraordinary—a giant clam shell that was slightly open. The shell was wider than his waist, and its surfaces shimmered with an unusual iridescence. Intrigued, he inserted a finger into it. The clam opened wider, revealing a space within. Jack reached in and felt something smooth and cool. It was a pearl!

He picked it up, feeling its weight and texture. The pearl was unlike any he had seen; it was large enough to hold water in its depths if it were carved into a bowl. Jack wondered if it might belong to the shell he'd discovered or could be worth something.
